page: Amazon Web Services t3.small is a burstable performance instance type belonging to the t3 family, built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2 virtual CPUs mapped to 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 2.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding 1.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the t3.small in the lower tier of evaluated cloud hardware, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance scores of 935.99 and 1364.45 respectively, both falling in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth is measured at 70.12 GB/sec for cached small blocks, dropping to 8.1 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Cryptographic and compression tests also reflect entry-level capabilities, with a Passmark CPU encryption score of 775.25 and single-threaded gzip compression throughput of 15.04 GB/sec.

The burstable CPU allocation model makes this instance highly cost-efficient for workloads with low baseline CPU utilization that require occasional bursts of higher performance. It is suited for lightweight applications such as basic static web serving, small-scale Redis caching, development environments, and low-traffic microservices.
description: Amazon Web Services t3.small is a burstable general-purpose instance designed for entry-level workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 2.5 GHz, it provides 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core and 2.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Benchmark results, including a Passmark CPU score of 1893.8 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 1364.45, place its performance in the bottom 10% of tested servers. This resource profile makes it a cost-efficient option for workloads with low baseline CPU requirements, such as static web serving, development environments, and lightweight microservices.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t3.small is a burstable instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 2.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor, designed for low-baseline workloads and development environments.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t3.small is a burstable instance with 2 vCPUs, 2.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, suited for low-baseline workloads.
tagline: An entry-level burstable compute instance designed for low-baseline workloads, development environments, testing, and lightweight microservices with variable resource demands.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 2 vCPUs and 2.0 GB DDR4 memory
Burstable CPU allocation model for cost-efficient resource scaling
Nitro hypervisor architecture with x86_64 instruction set support
Suited for static web serving, development environments, and small Redis caches
No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware included
